proposed_from.topic: Flagship computational upgrade of the banked bounded-manipulation RDD partial-ID result: replace the closed-form single-cutoff envelope by a strongly-polynomial min-cost-flow / total-unimodularity algorithm for sharp covariate-conditional manipulation bounds across many cutoff neighborhoods, with an explicit dual certificate recovering the parent interval as the one-cutoff case.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue artifact is the banked parent pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded/v1, whose kernel is a one-cutoff bounded-manipulation retained-plan interval. This revision does not repackage that theorem. Its new object is the many-neighborhood laminar slice graph with explicit carry arcs, extendable retained-mass increments, and a node-potential dual certificate; the parent appears only as Corollary 1 after deleting the carry layer at J=1.","published_axis":"The closest published causal paper is gerard_rokkanen_rothe_2020_rdbounds, which is one-cutoff and does not exhibit a many-neighborhood laminar flow certificate. The closest published computational comparator is rosenman_rajkumar_gauriot_slonim_2019_discrete_manipulation, which computes manipulated-RD bounds numerically rather than via an explicit TU min-cost-flow representation. bertanha_2020_many_thresholds and cattaneo_keele_titiunik_vazquezbare_2021_multicutoff study many thresholds/cutoffs under point identification, not manipulation partial identification. The epsilon-gap is therefore a simultaneous many-neighborhood manipulation-bound object with an exhibited graph certificate and parent-collapse dual, not merely a new proof of one-cutoff trimming."}
seed_list: Replace the parent's candidate-ratio LP family by a λ-indexed min-cost-flow certificate whose J=1 dual collapses to the parent bounded-manipulation interval.
Show that laminar many-neighborhood manipulated-RD constraints form a total-unimodular network matrix, yielding a strongly polynomial sharp-endpoint algorithm.

Characterize an open class where the joint many-neighborhood dual is strictly tighter than solving each cutoff neighborhood separately because one binding cell cap changes another neighborhood's active retained mass.
Attach a computable support-function representation of the many-neighborhood sharp bound to intersection-bound inference for pooled manipulation-robust RD endpoints.

seed_details.one_line: Replace the parent's candidate-ratio LP family by a λ-indexed min-cost-flow certificate whose J=1 dual collapses to the parent bounded-manipulation interval.

anchor_paper.author: Gerard, Rokkanen, and Rothe
anchor_paper.claim: One-cutoff manipulated-RD effects remain sharply bounded, with manipulation extent inferred from data.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: one can derive sharp bounds under a general model

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP is avoided only if the theorem names the flow certificate itself, not just another LP restatement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Gerard-Rokkanen-Rothe plus routine LP duality because the parent already solves endpoint LPs.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap is not duality in one LP; it is an explicit network-incidence/TU representation over a many-neighborhood sharp family with a recovered J=1 dual corner.

adversarial_referee.instance: Headline says flow, but no named focal object beyond 'algorithm'.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patched by naming the λ-indexed flow certificate V_lambda and exhibiting it in §9.

adversarial_referee.instance: GRR may already contain the needed transformed network structure.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Current anchor read supports sharp one-cutoff bounds, not a many-neighborhood network certificate or TU claim.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only proves a sufficient flow embedding, not exact equivalence.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 1 is stated as exact equality of the transformed value and the flow optimum.

seed_details.one_line: Show that laminar many-neighborhood manipulated-RD constraints form a total-unimodular network matrix, yielding a strongly polynomial sharp-endpoint algorithm.

anchor_paper.author: Bertanha
anchor_paper.claim: Many-threshold RD needs pooled estimands and extra structure away from the single-cutoff case.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: researchers often want to make inferences on more meaningful average treatment effects

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: T=2-to-general warning: stronger only because the proposed TU object changes the computational class, not because J>1 adds more indices.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine incidence-matrix observation once neighborhoods are laminar.
seed_details.rebuttal: If true, it is still theorem-level because no cited manipulated-RD paper promotes the sharp set to a network matrix or a strongly-polynomial algorithm.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could be mere LP simplification rather than a new characterization.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 2 is paired with Conjecture 1 so TU is attached to an exact sharp-set representation.

adversarial_referee.instance: Bertanha is many-threshold point ID, not manipulated RD.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Used as the many-neighborhood consumer/comparator, not as the manipulation comparator; GRR and Rosenman anchor the manipulation side.

adversarial_referee.instance: No explicit boundary case recovering the parent.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize an open class where the joint many-neighborhood dual is strictly tighter than solving each cutoff neighborhood separately because one binding cell cap changes another neighborhood's active retained mass.

anchor_paper.author: Rosenman, Rajkumar, Gauriot, and Slonim
anchor_paper.claim: Discrete manipulated-RD bounds can be computed by an optimization procedure, but the representation is numerical rather than a joint sharp dual certificate.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Our procedure is applicable in various regression discontinuity settings

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/open-class warning: stronger only if the strict-gain claim is promoted from one witness to an open class with a concrete nested-neighborhood exhibit.
seed_details.referee_objection: Any strict gain is a hand-tuned witness and disappears off that example.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed is written as an open-class dual-gain conjecture and the draft adds a concrete nested-neighborhood witness to avoid a trivial single-instance claim.

adversarial_referee.instance: Strict gap may sit on a single tuned mass table.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Exhibit 9.3 gives a non-corner nested-neighborhood witness; conjecture promotes it to an open class.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only upper endpoint discussed, not lower or support function.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Draft positions the pooled interval via V_lambda and flags the strict-gain statement at the endpoint level first.

adversarial_referee.instance: No computable object distinguishes joint and separate solutions.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The dual prices (alpha_j, eta_jw, nu_j) are named as the focal object in Conjecture 3.

seed_details.one_line: Attach a computable support-function representation of the many-neighborhood sharp bound to intersection-bound inference for pooled manipulation-robust RD endpoints.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Lee, and Rosen
anchor_paper.claim: Inference is available once the identified set is represented as an intersection-bound object.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: many bounds characterizations in econometrics ... can be formulated as intersection bounds

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Inference-only upgrade is weaker unless the identification object is first made computable; otherwise this is just downstream packaging.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is only a consumer paragraph, not a flagship theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; treated as a lower-ranked upgrade move, not the chosen kernel.

adversarial_referee.instance: No new identification object, only inference packaging.
CLR is generic and may not speak to RD manipulation geometry.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Used only as downstream consumer, not as the core novelty anchor.

adversarial_referee.instance: Would add inference without proving the flow representation.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: lands; keep below the chosen angle

proposed_from.literature_map:
Verified reused parent anchors: Gerard, Rokkanen, and Rothe (2020, Quantitative Economics) still states the core manipulated-RD fact that sharp bounds remain available under manipulation and that the manipulation extent is inferred from data; this is the parent theorem-level comparator.
Verified reused parent anchors: McCrary (2008, Journal of Econometrics) still supplies the density-discontinuity manipulation test; it is a diagnostic, not a sharp many-neighborhood computational certificate.
Verified reused parent anchors: Cattaneo, Jansson, and Ma (2020, JASA) still provide the practical local-polynomial density estimator that would feed the envelope parameters in applied work.
New computation-side anchor: Rosenman, Rajkumar, Gauriot, and Slonim (2019, arXiv) explicitly propose partial-identification bounds for manipulated discrete RD via optimization, but not a global flow/TU certificate.
New multi-cutoff anchor: Bertanha (2020, Journal of Econometrics) studies many-threshold RD and pooled estimands under point-identification assumptions, not manipulation-driven partial identification.
New inference anchor: Chernozhukov, Lee, and Rosen (2013, Econometrica) show how intersection-bound objects admit inference once the sharp bound is represented as a computable support function.
New software/practice anchor: Cattaneo, Titiunik, and Vazquez-Bare (2020, Stata Journal) document practical multi-cutoff RD workflows, which gives a direct consumer for a pooled manipulation-robust certificate.
Core tension carried forward from the gaps payload: one-cutoff sharp manipulated-RD bounds exist, and multi-cutoff RD estimands exist, but no cited paper turns the many-neighborhood manipulated-RD sharp set into a min-cost-flow or total-unimodular object.
Computation-side gap sharpened by the parent banked proposal: the current local route is generic candidate-ratio LP evaluation, not a reusable strongly-polynomial algorithm.
Proposal-level frontier: recover the parent interval as the J=1 dual corner while proving that laminar many-neighborhood systems share one sharp flow certificate rather than separate one-cutoff envelopes.

banked_reason: Stage -0.5 NO-PASS after 5 angles: RDD min-cost-flow computation upgrade had plausible literature gap but failed proposal review because finite endpoint search, retained-mass candidates, and active-budget dual certificate remained under-specified.
